    19. setDoubleValue(double)
    20. setCharValue(char)

Interface Variable<T>

Type Parameters:
T - the type of the variable
All Known Implementing Classes:
BooleanVariable, ByteVariable, CharVariable, DoubleVariable, FloatVariable, IntVariable, LongVariable, ObjectVariable, ShortVariable
public interface Variable<T>
Base interface for all variables stored within a sprite or a stage.

Note for Scratchers: This interface and its implementations match the functionality of the "Variables" block section in Scratch.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    Variable<T>
    copy()
    Creates and returns a shallow copy of this variable.
    T
    getValue()
    Returns the current value of this variable.
    default boolean
    getValueAsBoolean()
    Returns the current value as boolean, if possible, else throws an exception.
    default byte
    getValueAsByte()
    Returns the current value as byte, if possible, else throws an exception.
    default char
    getValueAsChar()
    Returns the current value as char, if possible, else throws an exception.
    default double
    getValueAsDouble()
    Returns the current value as double, if possible, else throws an exception.
    default float
    getValueAsFloat()
    Returns the current value as float, if possible, else throws an exception.
    default int
    getValueAsInt()
    Returns the current value as int, if possible, else throws an exception.
    default long
    getValueAsLong()
    Returns the current value as long, if possible, else throws an exception.
    default short
    getValueAsShort()
    Returns the current value as short, if possible, else throws an exception.
    boolean
    is(Class<?> cls)
    Returns whether the current value of this variable can be exactly represented as the class represented by the given Class object.
    default void
    setBooleanValue(boolean newValue)
    Sets the current value as boolean (optional operation).
    default void
    setByteValue(byte newValue)
    Sets the current value as byte (optional operation).
    default void
    setCharValue(char newValue)
    Sets the current value as char (optional operation).
    default void
    setDoubleValue(double newValue)
    Sets the current value as double (optional operation).
    default void
    setFloatValue(float newValue)
    Sets the current value as float (optional operation).
    default void
    setIntValue(int newValue)
    Sets the current value as int (optional operation).
    default void
    setLongValue(long newValue)
    Sets the current value as long (optional operation).
    default void
    setShortValue(short newValue)
    Sets the current value as short (optional operation).
    void
    setValue(T newValue)
    Sets the value of this variable to the given one.

  • Method Details

    • getValue

      T getValue()
      Returns the current value of this variable.
      Returns:
      the value

    • setValue

      void setValue(T newValue)
      Sets the value of this variable to the given one.
      Parameters:
      newValue - the new value

    • is

      boolean is(Class<?> cls)
      Returns whether the current value of this variable can be exactly represented as the class represented by the given Class object. Primitive classes should also be accepted.
      Parameters:
      cls - the class
      Returns:
      true if the current value is an instance of the given class, false otherwise
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if the given class object is null (optional)

    • copy

      Variable<T> copy()
      Creates and returns a shallow copy of this variable. The copy should not reflect changes to the source Variable, however, the value's state may be shared.
      Returns:
      a copy of this variable

    • getValueAsBoolean

      default boolean getValueAsBoolean()
      Returns the current value as boolean, if possible, else throws an exception.
      Returns:
      the current value as boolean if applicable
      Throws:
      ClassCastException - if current value can not be represented as boolean

    • getValueAsByte

      default byte getValueAsByte()
      Returns the current value as byte, if possible, else throws an exception.
      Returns:
      the current value as byte if applicable
      Throws:
      ClassCastException - if current value can not be represented as byte

    • getValueAsShort

      default short getValueAsShort()
      Returns the current value as short, if possible, else throws an exception.
      Returns:
      the current value as short if applicable
      Throws:
      ClassCastException - if current value can not be represented as short

    • getValueAsInt

      default int getValueAsInt()
      Returns the current value as int, if possible, else throws an exception.
      Returns:
      the current value as int if applicable
      Throws:
      ClassCastException - if current value can not be represented as int

    • getValueAsLong

      default long getValueAsLong()
      Returns the current value as long, if possible, else throws an exception.
      Returns:
      the current value as long if applicable
      Throws:
      ClassCastException - if current value can not be represented as long

    • getValueAsFloat

      default float getValueAsFloat()
      Returns the current value as float, if possible, else throws an exception.
      Returns:
      the current value as float if applicable
      Throws:
      ClassCastException - if current value can not be represented as float

    • getValueAsDouble

      default double getValueAsDouble()
      Returns the current value as double, if possible, else throws an exception.
      Returns:
      the current value as double if applicable
      Throws:
      ClassCastException - if current value can not be represented as double

    • getValueAsChar

      default char getValueAsChar()
      Returns the current value as char, if possible, else throws an exception.
      Returns:
      the current value as char if applicable
      Throws:
      ClassCastException - if current value can not be represented as char

    • setBooleanValue

      default void setBooleanValue(boolean newValue)
      Sets the current value as boolean (optional operation).
      Parameters:
      newValue - the new boolean value
      Throws:
      UnsupportedOperationException - if setting the value as boolean is not supported
      "Implementation Note:"
      The default implementation just throws an UnsupportedOperationException.

    • setByteValue

      default void setByteValue(byte newValue)
      Sets the current value as byte (optional operation).
      Parameters:
      newValue - the new byte value
      Throws:
      UnsupportedOperationException - if setting the value as byte is not supported
      "Implementation Note:"
      The default implementation just throws an UnsupportedOperationException.

    • setShortValue

      default void setShortValue(short newValue)
      Sets the current value as short (optional operation).
      Parameters:
      newValue - the new short value
      Throws:
      UnsupportedOperationException - if setting the value as short is not supported
      "Implementation Note:"
      The default implementation just throws an UnsupportedOperationException.

    • setIntValue

      default void setIntValue(int newValue)
      Sets the current value as int (optional operation).
      Parameters:
      newValue - the new int value
      Throws:
      UnsupportedOperationException - if setting the value as int is not supported
      "Implementation Note:"
      The default implementation just throws an UnsupportedOperationException.

    • setLongValue

      default void setLongValue(long newValue)
      Sets the current value as long (optional operation).
      Parameters:
      newValue - the new long value
      Throws:
      UnsupportedOperationException - if setting the value as long is not supported
      "Implementation Note:"
      The default implementation just throws an UnsupportedOperationException.

      default void setFloatValue(float newValue)
      Sets the current value as float (optional operation).
      Parameters:

      Throws:
      UnsupportedOperationException - if setting the value as float is not supported

    • setDoubleValue

      default void setDoubleValue(double newValue)
      Sets the current value as double (optional operation).
      Parameters:
      newValue - the new double value
      Throws:
      UnsupportedOperationException - if setting the value as double is not supported
      "Implementation Note:"
      The default implementation just throws an UnsupportedOperationException.

    • setCharValue

      default void setCharValue(char newValue)
      Sets the current value as char (optional operation).
      Parameters:
      newValue - the new char value
      Throws:
      UnsupportedOperationException - if setting the value as char is not supported
      "Implementation Note:"
